title: cistus creticus subsp. corsicus
intro: Cistus creticus subsp. corsicus, also known as Corsican Rockrose or Crete Rockrose, is a species of flowering plant in the rockrose family (Cistaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for various skin issues and injuries. Cistus creticus subsp. corsicus is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
